rationale: I split Chemical & Bioengineering into 14 practitioner-facing subspecialties to balance breadth (process/chemical side) and depth (biotech/biomedical side) while keeping each card large enough to be useful. I kept process- and reaction-focused work (Process Engineering, Catalysis, Separation) distinct from biomolecular and cell-based areas (Bioprocessing, Synthetic Biology, Tissue Engineering) because authors and readers identify with those communities. I deliberately left pure/analytical chemistry and clinical device deployment out — those go to Chemistry and Medicine respectively — and I combined some closely coupled topics (Catalysis & Reaction Engineering) rather than splitting them into narrowly overlapping niches to keep the grid MECE and practical for selection by working professionals.

subspecialties.name: Process Engineering
subspecialties.tagline: Scaling, design and optimization of chemical/biotech processes
covers: plant design, process modelling, scale-up, safety and economics
example: reactor network optimization, process intensification studies
explicitly_excludes: separation-&-environmental-process-engineering — unit operations like separations and wastewater focus
process-chemistry — bench-scale synthetic route development

subspecialties.name: Chemical Engineering
subspecialties.tagline: Fundamental transport, thermodynamics, and reaction systems
covers: heat/mass transfer, fluid mechanics, multiphase flows, reactor fundamentals
example: CFD for reactors, transport models for porous media
explicitly_excludes: process-engineering — plant-scale design and economics
catalysis-&-reaction-engineering — catalyst design and kinetics focus

subspecialties.name: Process Chemistry
subspecialties.tagline: Applied synthetic chemistry for scale and manufacturing
covers: route scouting, scale-up of organic/inorganic syntheses, impurity control
example: manufacturing route optimization for APIs, telescoped syntheses
explicitly_excludes: chemistry — fundamental synthetic methods, mechanistic organic chemistry
bioprocessing-&-fermentation — biological production routes

subspecialties.name: Bioprocessing & Fermentation
subspecialties.tagline: Upstream/downstream processes for biologics and fermentation products
covers: cell culture, fermentation engineering, downstream purification, scale-up
example: CHO cell fed-batch optimization, protein A chromatography scale-up
explicitly_excludes: synthetic-biology — genetic design and circuit engineering
process-engineering — plant-level integration beyond bioprocess unit ops

subspecialties.name: Synthetic Biology
subspecialties.tagline: Genetic circuits, DNA tools, and engineered biological systems
covers: design of genetic parts, chassis engineering, modular biological systems
example: CRISPR-based circuits, standardized genetic part libraries
explicitly_excludes: metabolic-engineering — pathway optimization for product titer
biomedical-engineering — device and implant-focused work

subspecialties.name: Metabolic Engineering
subspecialties.tagline: Pathway-level redesigns to produce chemicals, fuels, and biomolecules
covers: flux analysis, pathway optimization, strain engineering for production
example: pathway balancing for commodity chemical biosynthesis
explicitly_excludes: synthetic-biology — tools and circuits not aimed at production metrics
bioprocessing-&-fermentation — downstream purification and unit ops

subspecialties.name: Biomaterials
subspecialties.tagline: Materials for interacting with biology: implants, scaffolds, coatings
covers: design, synthesis and characterization of materials for biomedical use
example: bioactive coatings, hydrogels for cell support, degradable polymers
explicitly_excludes: tissue-engineering — construct/organ-level regenerative solutions
polymer-science — fundamental polymer chemistry and physics

subspecialties.name: Tissue Engineering
subspecialties.tagline: Engineered tissues, organoids and regenerative constructs
covers: scaffold design, cell–matrix interactions, organoid development
example: vascularized tissue constructs, stem-cell based organoids
explicitly_excludes: biomaterials — material development without tissue construct focus
medicine — clinical trials and device implantation studies

subspecialties.name: Drug Delivery
subspecialties.tagline: Formulation and engineered systems for delivering therapeutics
covers: nanocarriers, sustained release systems, targeting strategies
example: lipid nanoparticle formulation, polymeric depot systems
explicitly_excludes: pharmacology/medicine — clinical efficacy and patient trials
biomaterials — material development not focused on therapeutic delivery

subspecialties.name: Biomedical Engineering
subspecialties.tagline: Devices, sensors, and systems for health and diagnostics
covers: medical device design, biosensors, medical imaging engineering
example: wearable biosensors, implantable electronics (preclinical)
explicitly_excludes: medicine — clinical deployment, regulatory approval and patient care
microfluidics — primarily lab-on-chip device fabrication and assays

subspecialties.name: Microfluidics
covers: chip fabrication, droplet assays, microreactor chemistry and biology
example: single-cell droplet sequencing platforms, microreactor synthesis
explicitly_excludes: biomedical-engineering — macro medical devices and clinical systems
chemical-engineering — bulk transport modelling at process scale

subspecialties.name: Polymer Science
subspecialties.tagline: Synthesis, characterization and processing of polymers
covers: polymer chemistry, rheology, processing, functional polymer design
example: stimuli-responsive polymers, polymerization mechanism studies
explicitly_excludes: biomaterials — application-driven biomaterial studies for biology/medicine
chemistry — fundamental small-molecule synthetic chemistry

subspecialties.name: Catalysis & Reaction Engineering
subspecialties.tagline: Catalyst design, kinetics, and reactor-level reaction control
covers: heterogeneous/homogeneous catalysis, kinetics, catalytic reactor design
example: supported nanoparticle catalysts, kinetic modelling of catalytic flows
explicitly_excludes: chemical-engineering — transport-dominated reactor modelling without catalyst focus
chemistry — mechanistic organometallic studies not aimed at engineering

subspecialties.name: Separation & Environmental Process Engineering
subspecialties.tagline: Separations, membranes, and environmental treatment processes
covers: distillation, adsorption, membrane technology, wastewater treatment
example: membrane process design, adsorption for contaminant removal
explicitly_excludes: process-engineering — overall plant integration beyond separations
environmental-science — ecological impacts and field monitoring studies

uncovered_concerns: Single-cell engineering and analytical workflows: important but generally tagged under Microfluidics or Synthetic Biology depending on focus; avoided a separate 'single-cell' card to prevent fragmentation.
AI/ML for chemical and bioengineering: widely used as methods across topics rather than a discipline title, so not its own card (papers should map to the substantive area plus 'uses ML').
Regulatory science and techno-economic analysis: cross-cutting and often part of Process Engineering or Bioprocessing rather than separate specialty.
Clinical translation of devices: excluded on purpose and routed to Medicine because practitioner identity shifts to clinical/medical domain.
